rationale: Determinism check is mathematically unprovable. The function's ensures clause only constrains ret.1 when ret.0 is false. When r1.0 == r2.0 == true (forall holds), the antecedent leaves r1.1 and r2.1 unconstrained, so r1=(true,true) with r2=(true,false) satisfies the antecedent yet falsifies det_keys_in_index_range_agree_equal(r1, r2). I verified r1.0==r2.0 (always) and r1.1==r2.1 (under !r1.0) hold as tautological lemmas, but the overall implication is not a theorem. The sibling values_agree determinism check has the same fundamental flaw and also failed.
